patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH] btrfs: btrfs_log_dev_io_error() on all bio errors

version_history.subject: [PATCH] btrfs: btrfs_log_dev_io_error() on all bio errors

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch modifies the btrfs_log_dev_io_error() function to log device I/O errors for all bio error status codes, not just BLK_STS_IOERR and BLK_STS_TARGET. This change is intended to prevent incorrect conclusions about system behavior when encountering unexpected bio failure status values.

review_comments.author: Qu Wenruo
review_comments.summary: Approved the patch with a Reviewed-by tag, indicating it meets code quality standards and is suitable for merge.
review_comments.raw_body:
\u5728 2026/3/14 05:56, Boris Burkov \u5199\u9053:
> As far as I can tell, we never intentionally constrained ourselves to
> these status codes, and it is misleading and surprising to lack the
> bdev error logging when we get a different error code from the block
> layer. This can lead to jumping to a wrong conclusion like "this
> system didn't see any bio failures but aborted with EIO".
> 
> For example on nvme devices, I observe many failures coming back as
> BLK_STS_MEDIUM. It is apparent that the nvme driver returns a variety of
> BLK_STS_* status values in nvme_error_status().
> 
> Signed-off-by: Boris Burkov <boris@bur.io>

Reviewed-by: Qu Wenruo <wqu@suse.com>

Thanks,
Qu


> ---
>   fs/btrfs/bio.c | 3 +--
>   1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 2 deletions(-)
> 
> diff --git a/fs/btrfs/bio.c b/fs/btrfs/bio.c
> index 2a2a21aec817..08b1d62603d0 100644
> --- a/fs/btrfs/bio.c
> +++ b/fs/btrfs/bio.c
> @@ -352,8 +352,7 @@ static void btrfs_log_dev_io_error(const struct bio *bio, struct btrfs_device *d
>   {
>   	if (!dev || !dev->bdev)
>   		return;
> -	if (bio->bi_status != BLK_STS_IOERR && bio->bi_status != BLK_STS_TARGET)
> -		return;
> +	ASSERT(bio->bi_status);
>   
>   	if (btrfs_op(bio) == BTRFS_MAP_WRITE)
>   		btrfs_dev_stat_inc_and_print(dev, BTRFS_DEV_STAT_WRITE_ERRS);

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v2 0/2] vfs: follow-on fixes for i_ino widening

version_history.subject: [PATCH 0/2] vfs: follow-on fixes for i_ino widening
[PATCH v2 0/2] vfs: follow-on fixes for i_ino widening

series_items.subject: [PATCH v2 1/2] nilfs2: fix 64-bit division operations in nilfs_bmap_find_target_in_group()
series_items.patch_summary: The patch fixes a build failure on 32-bit ARM caused by the introduction of 64-bit inode numbers, which triggers undefined behavior in the `__aeabi_uldivmod` function. The fix involves using 64-bit division interfaces to calculate the group number and index within that group.

review_comments.author: David Laight
review_comments.summary: Raised concerns that `entries_per_group` might not be a 64-bit value and suggested using a simpler approach to calculate the index within the group, which would also improve performance.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Fri, 13 Mar 2026 14:45:20 -0400
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org> wrote:

> With the change to make inode->i_ino a u64, the build started failing on
> 32-bit ARM with:
> 
>     ERROR: modpost: "__aeabi_uldivmod" [fs/nilfs2/nilfs2.ko] undefined!
> 
> Fix this by using the 64-bit division interfaces in
> nilfs_bmap_find_target_in_group().
> 
> Fixes: 998a59d371c2 ("treewide: fix missed i_ino format specifier conversions")
> Reported-by: kernel test robot <lkp@intel.com>
> Closes: https://lore.kernel.org/oe-kbuild-all/202603100602.KPxiClIO-lkp@intel.com/
> Reviewed-by: Viacheslav Dubeyko <slava@dubeyko.com>
> Acked-by: Ryusuke Konishi <konishi.ryusuke@gmail.com>
> Signed-off-by: Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>
> ---
>  fs/nilfs2/bmap.c | 9 ++++++---
>  1 file changed, 6 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)
> 
> diff --git a/fs/nilfs2/bmap.c b/fs/nilfs2/bmap.c
> index 824f2bd91c167965ec3a660202b6e6c5f1fe007e..abcf5252578ad24f694bfccf525893674bfcb4bc 100644
> --- a/fs/nilfs2/bmap.c
> +++ b/fs/nilfs2/bmap.c
> @@ -455,11 +455,14 @@ __u64 nilfs_bmap_find_target_in_group(const struct nilfs_bmap *bmap)
>  {
>  	struct inode *dat = nilfs_bmap_get_dat(bmap);
>  	unsigned long entries_per_group = nilfs_palloc_entries_per_group(dat);
> -	unsigned long group = bmap->b_inode->i_ino / entries_per_group;

Are you sure entries_per_group can be more than 32 bits?
It looks like something that will be the same size on 32 and 64bit.

> +	unsigned long group;
> +	u32 index;
> +
> +	group = div_u64(bmap->b_inode->i_ino, entries_per_group);

You don't need the full 64 by 64 divide.
IIRC there are both div_u64_u32() and div_u64_ulong().

> +	div_u64_rem(bmap->b_inode->i_ino, NILFS_BMAP_GROUP_DIV, &index);

NILFD_BMAP_GROUP_DIV is 8 (and probably has to be a power of 2).
So:
	index = bmap->b_inode->i_ino & (NILFS_BMAP_GROUP_DIV - 1);
is the same and likely much faster to calculate.
(The compiler will have done that optimisation before.)

	David


>  
>  	return group * entries_per_group +
> -		(bmap->b_inode->i_ino % NILFS_BMAP_GROUP_DIV) *
> -		(entries_per_group / NILFS_BMAP_GROUP_DIV);
> +	       index * (entries_per_group / NILFS_BMAP_GROUP_DIV);
>  }
>  
>  static struct lock_class_key nilfs_bmap_dat_lock_key;
>

review_comments.author: Jeff Layton
review_comments.summary: Acknowledged David's concerns and suggested that the NILFS2 maintainers should provide guidance on how to proceed with the patch.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Sat, 2026-03-14 at 12:47 +0000, David Laight wrote:
> On Fri, 13 Mar 2026 14:45:20 -0400
> Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org> wrote:
> 
> > With the change to make inode->i_ino a u64, the build started failing on
> > 32-bit ARM with:
> > 
> >     ERROR: modpost: "__aeabi_uldivmod" [fs/nilfs2/nilfs2.ko] undefined!
> > 
> > Fix this by using the 64-bit division interfaces in
> > nilfs_bmap_find_target_in_group().
> > 
> > Fixes: 998a59d371c2 ("treewide: fix missed i_ino format specifier conversions")
> > Reported-by: kernel test robot <lkp@intel.com>
> > Closes: https://lore.kernel.org/oe-kbuild-all/202603100602.KPxiClIO-lkp@intel.com/
> > Reviewed-by: Viacheslav Dubeyko <slava@dubeyko.com>
> > Acked-by: Ryusuke Konishi <konishi.ryusuke@gmail.com>
> > Signed-off-by: Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>
> > ---
> >  fs/nilfs2/bmap.c | 9 ++++++---
> >  1 file changed, 6 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)
> > 
> > diff --git a/fs/nilfs2/bmap.c b/fs/nilfs2/bmap.c
> > index 824f2bd91c167965ec3a660202b6e6c5f1fe007e..abcf5252578ad24f694bfccf525893674bfcb4bc 100644
> > --- a/fs/nilfs2/bmap.c
> > +++ b/fs/nilfs2/bmap.c
> > @@ -455,11 +455,14 @@ __u64 nilfs_bmap_find_target_in_group(const struct nilfs_bmap *bmap)
> >  {
> >  	struct inode *dat = nilfs_bmap_get_dat(bmap);
> >  	unsigned long entries_per_group = nilfs_palloc_entries_per_group(dat);
> > -	unsigned long group = bmap->b_inode->i_ino / entries_per_group;
> 
> Are you sure entries_per_group can be more than 32 bits?
> It looks like something that will be the same size on 32 and 64bit.
> 

I'm not sure of anything here. I'm just want to get this to compile on
all arches. FWIW, I'm not looking to optimize anything in this patch.

> > +	unsigned long group;
> > +	u32 index;
> > +
> > +	group = div_u64(bmap->b_inode->i_ino, entries_per_group);
> 
> You don't need the full 64 by 64 divide.
> IIRC there are both div_u64_u32() and div_u64_ulong().
>
> > +	div_u64_rem(bmap->b_inode->i_ino, NILFS_BMAP_GROUP_DIV, &index);
> 
> NILFD_BMAP_GROUP_DIV is 8 (and probably has to be a power of 2).
> So:
> 	index = bmap->b_inode->i_ino & (NILFS_BMAP_GROUP_DIV - 1);
> is the same and likely much faster to calculate.
> (The compiler will have done that optimisation before.)
> 
> 

That all sounds reasonable to me. At this point though, it would be
better if the NILFS2 folks stepped in with how they'd prefer this be
done.

> 
> >  
> >  	return group * entries_per_group +
> > -		(bmap->b_inode->i_ino % NILFS_BMAP_GROUP_DIV) *
> > -		(entries_per_group / NILFS_BMAP_GROUP_DIV);
> > +	       index * (entries_per_group / NILFS_BMAP_GROUP_DIV);
> >  }
> >  
> >  static struct lock_class_key nilfs_bmap_dat_lock_key;
> > 

-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

series_items.subject: [PATCH v2 2/2] EVM: add comment describing why ino field is still unsigned long
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds a comment to the EVM code explaining why the inode number field remains an unsigned long, allowing existing HMAC and signatures from 32-bit hosts to continue working when the inode hasn't changed and fits in a u32.

review_comments.author: Mimi Zohar
review_comments.summary: Expressed gratitude for Jeff Layton's patch and applied the Reviewed-by tag.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Fri, 2026-03-13 at 14:45 -0400, Jeff Layton wrote:
> Mimi pointed out that we didn't widen the inode number field in struct
> h_misc alongside the inode->i_ino widening. While we could make an
> equivalent change there, that would require EVM resigning on all 32-bit
> hosts.
> 
> Instead, leave the field as an unsigned long. This should have no effect
> on 64-bit hosts, and allow things to continue working on 32-bit hosts in
> the cases where the i_ino fits in 32-bits.
> 
> Add a comment explaining why it's being left as unsigned long.
> 
> Cc: Mimi Zohar <zohar@linux.ibm.com>
> Signed-off-by: Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

Thanks, Jeff.

Reviewed-by: Mimi Zohar <zohar@linux.ibm.com>


> ---
>  security/integrity/evm/evm_crypto.c | 6 ++++++
>  1 file changed, 6 insertions(+)
> 
> diff --git a/security/integrity/evm/evm_crypto.c b/security/integrity/evm/evm_crypto.c
> index c0ca4eedb0fe5d5c30f45f515a4bc90248ec64ea..1c41af2f91a60a714878ff93b554c90e45546503 100644
> --- a/security/integrity/evm/evm_crypto.c
> +++ b/security/integrity/evm/evm_crypto.c
> @@ -144,6 +144,12 @@ static void hmac_add_misc(struct shash_desc *desc, struct inode *inode,
>  			  char type, char *digest)
>  {
>  	struct h_misc {
> +		/*
> +		 * Although inode->i_ino is now u64, this field remains
> +		 * unsigned long to allow existing HMAC and signatures from
> +		 * 32-bit hosts to continue working when i_ino hasn't changed
> +		 * and fits in a u32.
> +		 */
>  		unsigned long ino;
>  		__u32 generation;
>  		uid_t uid;

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch series addresses follow-on issues related to inode->i_ino widening, specifically fixing division operations in nilfs2 and adding a comment for EVM. The patches aim to ensure compatibility across different architectures.

review_comments.author: David Laight
review_comments.summary: Raised concerns about the division operation in nilfs2, suggesting a simpler approach using div_u64_u32() and optimizing the calculation of index. Also pointed out that entries_per_group should not exceed 32 bits.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Fri, 13 Mar 2026 14:45:20 -0400
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org> wrote:

> With the change to make inode->i_ino a u64, the build started failing on
> 32-bit ARM with:
> 
>     ERROR: modpost: "__aeabi_uldivmod" [fs/nilfs2/nilfs2.ko] undefined!
> 
> Fix this by using the 64-bit division interfaces in
> nilfs_bmap_find_target_in_group().
> 
> Fixes: 998a59d371c2 ("treewide: fix missed i_ino format specifier conversions")
> Reported-by: kernel test robot <lkp@intel.com>
> Closes: https://lore.kernel.org/oe-kbuild-all/202603100602.KPxiClIO-lkp@intel.com/
> Reviewed-by: Viacheslav Dubeyko <slava@dubeyko.com>
> Acked-by: Ryusuke Konishi <konishi.ryusuke@gmail.com>
> Signed-off-by: Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>
> ---
>  fs/nilfs2/bmap.c | 9 ++++++---
>  1 file changed, 6 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)
> 
> diff --git a/fs/nilfs2/bmap.c b/fs/nilfs2/bmap.c
> index 824f2bd91c167965ec3a660202b6e6c5f1fe007e..abcf5252578ad24f694bfccf525893674bfcb4bc 100644
> --- a/fs/nilfs2/bmap.c
> +++ b/fs/nilfs2/bmap.c
> @@ -455,11 +455,14 @@ __u64 nilfs_bmap_find_target_in_group(const struct nilfs_bmap *bmap)
>  {
>  	struct inode *dat = nilfs_bmap_get_dat(bmap);
>  	unsigned long entries_per_group = nilfs_palloc_entries_per_group(dat);
> -	unsigned long group = bmap->b_inode->i_ino / entries_per_group;

Are you sure entries_per_group can be more than 32 bits?
It looks like something that will be the same size on 32 and 64bit.

> +	unsigned long group;
> +	u32 index;
> +
> +	group = div_u64(bmap->b_inode->i_ino, entries_per_group);

You don't need the full 64 by 64 divide.
IIRC there are both div_u64_u32() and div_u64_ulong().

> +	div_u64_rem(bmap->b_inode->i_ino, NILFS_BMAP_GROUP_DIV, &index);

NILFD_BMAP_GROUP_DIV is 8 (and probably has to be a power of 2).
So:
	index = bmap->b_inode->i_ino & (NILFS_BMAP_GROUP_DIV - 1);
is the same and likely much faster to calculate.
(The compiler will have done that optimisation before.)

	David


>  
>  	return group * entries_per_group +
> -		(bmap->b_inode->i_ino % NILFS_BMAP_GROUP_DIV) *
> -		(entries_per_group / NILFS_BMAP_GROUP_DIV);
> +	       index * (entries_per_group / NILFS_BMAP_GROUP_DIV);
>  }
>  
>  static struct lock_class_key nilfs_bmap_dat_lock_key;
>

review_comments.author: Jeff Layton
review_comments.summary: Acknowledged David's concerns and suggested that the NILFS2 team should provide guidance on how to proceed with the patch.

patches_reviewed.subject: Re: [PATCH v2 1/2] nilfs2: fix 64-bit division operations in nilfs_bmap_find_target_in_group()
patches_reviewed.patch_summary: Patch author seeks review and potential input from NILFS2 maintainers for a patch fixing 64-bit division operations in nilfs_bmap_find_target_in_group().

review_comments.author: Jeff Layton
review_comments.summary: Author seeks review and input from NILFS2 maintainers, but expresses uncertainty about the patch's correctness and suggests deferring to them for guidance.

patches_reviewed.subject: Re: [PATCH v7 2/2] NFSD: convert callback RPC program to per-net namespace
patches_reviewed.patch_summary: This patch simplifies the NFSD callback RPC program by embedding its struct directly within the nfsd_net structure, eliminating the need for a separate allocation.

review_comments.author: Chuck Lever
review_comments.summary: Reviewer Chuck Lever noted that embedding the nfsd_cb struct inside nfsd_net would create a tight coupling between netns.h and the NFSv4 callback program, which he avoided by using a pointer to hide implementation details.
review_comments.raw_body:
The reason I did this is because otherwise netns.h would need
to have knowledge of the details of the NFSv4 callback program.
Things like the NFSv4 callback program's RPC program number
are implementation-defined...

I'm avoiding a weak layering violation by hiding those details
behind a pointer.


-- 
Chuck Lever

review_comments.author: Jeff Layton (author)
review_comments.summary: Author acknowledges that embedding nfsd_cb inside nfsd_net would have been a better approach, but considers it a minor issue and does not plan to restructure the code in this patch version.
review_comments.raw_body:
Meh. I'd probably have prioritized avoiding the extra allocation, but
that's a minor thing.

Reviewed-by: Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>
